title: Benthamia spiralis
intro: Benthamia spiralis is a flowering plant species commonly referred to as the spiral gentian or spiral gentian vine, belonging to the family Gentianaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Benthamia spiralis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Benthamia spiralis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The genus Benthamia belongs to the subfamily Vanilleae and is a part of the larger order Asparagales. Benthamia spiralis is native to Madagascar, where it is endemic. It is a small, epiphytic orchid with unique, twisted stems and flowers. This species was first described by the French botanist Jean-Baptiste Lemée in 1856.
